Description: Pages served over plain HTTP. HTTPS is a ranking signal and browsers mark HTTP pages as 'not secure'.
How To Fix: Serve all pages over HTTPS and 301 redirect HTTP to HTTPS.

Description: Pages with no links to other internal pages. Search engines have trouble discovering pages that are not linked, and PageRank cannot flow onwards.
How To Fix: Link to relevant internal pages to help users continue their journey and pass PageRank onwards. If links are only client-side, render them server-side.

Description: No XML sitemap found. Sitemaps are the primary discovery mechanism for search engines and many AI crawlers.
How To Fix: Generate a sitemap.xml of canonical URLs and reference it from robots.txt.

Description: Pages with no canonical URL (link element or HTTP header). Without one, search engines pick the version they consider best, which can be unpredictable when multiple versions exist.
How To Fix: Specify a canonical URL on every indexable page.

Description: No robots.txt found. Crawlers (search and AI) fall back to default behavior and you lose the ability to declare sitemaps and per-bot policies.
How To Fix: Add a robots.txt declaring crawl policy and the sitemap location.

Description: Pages whose titles exceed 60 characters. Characters over the limit may be truncated in Google's search results and carry less weight in scoring.
How To Fix: Write concise page titles so important words are not truncated in the SERPs.

Description: Pages without Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:image). Shares on social platforms and chat apps render without a rich preview, lowering CTR.
How To Fix: Add og:title, og:description, og:image and og:url meta tags.

Description: No llms.txt found. llms.txt is an emerging standard that gives LLMs and AI agents a curated, markdown map of your most important content - improving how AI systems summarize and cite the site.
How To Fix: Add /llms.txt with a short site description and links to key pages and docs.

Description: No Organization (or WebSite) schema found anywhere on the site. Entity-level markup helps AI systems attribute content to a verified organization (E-E-A-T).
How To Fix: Add Organization JSON-LD (name, url, logo, sameAs) on the homepage.

Description: Pages with multiple <h2>s. HTML standards allow this within a logical heading hierarchy; the filter helps review whether they are used appropriately.
How To Fix: Ensure <h2>s sit in a logical hierarchical structure, using h3-h6 for deeper levels where appropriate.

Description: URLs missing the Content-Security-Policy response header, which guards against cross-site scripting by controlling which resources may load.
How To Fix: Set a strict Content-Security-Policy response header across all pages.

Description: URLs missing the X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header, allowing browsers to MIME-sniff responses away from the declared content type.
How To Fix: Set X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on all responses.

Description: URLs missing X-Frame-Options (or CSP frame-ancestors), leaving pages embeddable in iframes and exposed to clickjacking.
How To Fix: Set X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN or a CSP frame-ancestors directive.

Description: URLs missing a secure Referrer-Policy (no-referrer-when-downgrade, strict-origin-when-cross-origin, no-referrer or strict-origin). Full URLs can leak cross-origin in the Referer header.
How To Fix: Set Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin.

Description: Pages with a missing, empty or whitespace meta description. This is a missed opportunity to influence click-through rates from search results.
How To Fix: Write unique, descriptive meta descriptions on key pages that communicate the purpose of the page and entice clicks.

Description: Section headings lack id attributes, so deep links to specific passages are impossible. Citable fragments improve how AI answers and SERP features link in.
How To Fix: Add stable id attributes to h2/h3 headings to enable deep links.

Description: Copy on the page is very difficult to read (Flesch reading-ease below 30) and is best understood by university graduates.
How To Fix: Use shorter sentences and simpler words to improve readability for your audience.

Description: Pages without BreadcrumbList schema. Breadcrumbs help search and answer engines understand site hierarchy and display better result paths.
How To Fix: Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD reflecting the page's position in the site.

Description: Pages without a twitter:card meta tag; X/Twitter shares fall back to a bare link.
How To Fix: Add <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image"> plus title, description and image tags.

Description: No RSS/Atom feed discovered. Feeds help aggregators and AI systems track fresh content and surface it quickly.
How To Fix: Publish an RSS/Atom feed and declare it with <link rel="alternate">.

Description: No favicon was discovered. Favicons appear in browser tabs, bookmarks and Google's mobile search results.
How To Fix: Add a favicon and declare it with a <link rel="icon"> element.
